task: I need to send a short update to a client about a revision delay. Can you help me draft a plain, professional note that explains the situation and gives a realistic next step? I also may need a second version for if they ask again later.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are two plain, professional options you can use.

**Version 1: initial update**
> Hi [Client Name],  
> I wanted to let you know that the revision is taking a bit longer than expected. I’m still working through it and want to make sure I get it right before sending it over.  
> I expect to have the updated version to you by [day/time]. Thanks for your patience.

**Version 2: if they ask again later**
> Hi [Client Name],  
> Quick update: the revision is still in progress, and I’m making sure everything is addressed properly before I send it back. I’m aiming to have it ready by [day/time].  
> I appreciate your patience and will keep you posted if anything changes.

If you want, I can also make these sound:
- more apologetic
- more confident/brief
- more warm and client-friendly

user_simulator.text:
Close, but I’d want it a little less wordy and a little more specific. The second one especially feels kind of generic.

Can you tighten both up and make them sound more like a real work update — plain, calm, no over-explaining. Maybe one version for first notice, one for a follow-up if they press again. And keep it neutral, not too apologetic.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are tighter, more work-like versions:

**1) First notice**
> Hi [Client Name], just a quick update: the revision is running behind schedule. I’m working through it now and expect to send the updated version by [day/time].

**2) Follow-up if they ask again**
> Hi [Client Name], the revision is still in progress. I’m on track to send it by [day/time], and I’ll let you know if that changes.

If you want, I can also make them:
- a little firmer
- a little warmer
- even shorter, like Slack/email one-liners

user_simulator.text:
Better. Still a little generic though — “running behind schedule” and “still in progress” are fine, but I’d want a bit more real-world detail without making it a whole explanation.

Something like:

1) First notice
Hi [Client Name] — quick update: I’m still working through the revision, but it’s taking a bit longer than planned. I expect to send the updated version by [day/time].

2) Follow-up
Hi [Client Name] — still on this. I’m finishing up the revision and expect to have it back to you by [day/time].

If they’ve already asked twice, I’d probably want the second one to sound a touch firmer, like I’m actively on it, not just floating an estimate.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are tightened versions with a more natural work-update feel:

**1) First notice**
> Hi [Client Name] — quick update: I’m still working through the revision, but it’s taking a bit longer than planned. I expect to send the updated version by [day/time].

**2) Follow-up if they ask again**
> Hi [Client Name] — still on this and finishing up the revision now. I expect to have it back to you by [day/time].

If you want, I can also give you:
- a slightly firmer version for the second one
- a version that sounds more polished/email-like
- a very short Slack-style version

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s basically it. The second one reads more active now, which is what I wanted. Good enough for a real client note.
